free_text: Neutron and gamma rays from the alpha-particle
bombardment of 9Be, 10B, 11B, 13C and 18O
Reaction code changed in sub.012,013
Target was about 10 keV thick to 2 MeV alphas
The neutron detecter used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.

Target was about 10 keV thick to 2 MeV alphas
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
Estimated error in cross sections.
Gamma-ray cross sections were obtained by using the
yield of 4.4-MeV gamma-rays from standardized RaD-Be
source assuming 0.6 gamma-rays per neutrons.
Fig. 1
Target was about 10 keV thick to 2 MeV alphas
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
Estimated error in cross sections.
Gamma-ray cross sections were obtained by using the
yield of 4.4-MeV gamma-rays from standardized RaD-Be
source assuming 0.6 gamma-rays per neutrons.
Table 1
Table 1
67% enriched 13C target was 20 keV thick for
3.0 MeV alphas
The neutron detecter used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
It is estimated that at most 10% of the gamma-ray
yield could be caused by neutrons in the NaI crystal.
The cross section calibration was made at 1.9-MeV
resonance using both weighed evaporated Be targets
on 0.002 in. aluminum foil and a thick Be target. A
stopping power of 1.40 MeV/mg/cm2 was used for 1.9 MeV
alphas in 9Be.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error

Table 4
Table 4
The targets were prepared by heating a tungsten disk
in induction heater with a sample of enriched 18O (40%)
The target thickness varies from 90 keV to 130keV.
The neutron detecter used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
The cross section wre obtained by estimating the
amount of oxygen in the target from the yield
obtained in measuring the 16O(d,n) threshold.
